root_text.an10.44:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kusinārāyaṁ viharati baliharaṇe vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.an10.44:2.1: “Codakena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ paccavekkhitvā pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā paro codetabbo.
root_text.an10.44:2.3: Codakena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ:
root_text.an10.44:2.4: ‘parisuddhakāyasamācāro nu khomhi, parisuddhenamhi kāyasamācārena samannāgato acchiddena appaṭimaṁsena.
root_text.an10.44:2.5: Saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti?
root_text.an10.44:2.6: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu parisuddhakāyasamācāro hoti parisuddhena kāyasamācārena samannāgato acchiddena appaṭimaṁsena, tassa bhavanti vattāro:
root_text.an10.44:2.7: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā kāyikaṁ sikkhassū’ti, itissa bhavanti vattāro.
root_text.an10.44:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, codakena bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ:
root_text.an10.44:3.2: ‘parisuddhavacīsamācāro nu khomhi, parisuddhenamhi vacīsamācārena samannāgato acchiddena appaṭimaṁsena.
root_text.an10.44:3.3: Saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti?
root_text.an10.44:3.4: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu parisuddhavacīsamācāro hoti parisuddhena vacīsamācārena samannāgato acchiddena appaṭimaṁsena, tassa bhavanti vattāro:
root_text.an10.44:3.5: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā vācasikaṁ sikkhassū’ti, itissa bhavanti vattāro.
root_text.an10.44:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, codakena bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ:
root_text.an10.44:4.2: ‘mettaṁ nu kho me cittaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ sabrahmacārīsu anāghātaṁ.
root_text.an10.44:4.3: Saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti?
root_text.an10.44:4.4: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno mettaṁ cittaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ hoti sabrahmacārīsu anāghātaṁ, tassa bhavanti vattāro:
root_text.an10.44:4.5: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā sabrahmacārīsu mettaṁ cittaṁ upaṭṭhāpehī’ti, itissa bhavanti vattāro.
root_text.an10.44:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, codakena bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ:
root_text.an10.44:5.2: ‘bahussuto nu khomhi sutadharo sutasannicayo, ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti, tathārūpā me dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an10.44:5.3: Saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti?
root_text.an10.44:5.4: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhu bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo, ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti, tathārūpāssa dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā, tassa bhavanti vattāro:
root_text.an10.44:5.5: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā āgamaṁ pariyāpuṇassū’ti, itissa bhavanti vattāro.
root_text.an10.44:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, codakena bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena evaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ:
root_text.an10.44:6.2: ‘ubhayāni kho pana me pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso.
root_text.an10.44:6.3: Saṁvijjati nu kho me eso dhammo udāhu no’ti?
root_text.an10.44:6.4: No ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ubhayāni pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso, ‘idaṁ panāyasmā, kattha vuttaṁ bhagavatā’ti, iti puṭṭho na sampāyissati.
root_text.an10.44:6.6: ‘iṅgha tāva āyasmā vinayaṁ sikkhassū’ti, itissa bhavanti vattāro.
root_text.an10.44:7.8: Codakena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā paraṁ codetukāmena ime pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ paccavekkhitvā ime pañca dhamme ajjhattaṁ upaṭṭhāpetvā paro codetabbo”ti.

translation_text.an10.44:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kusinārā, in the Forest of Offerings.
translation_text.an10.44:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an10.44:2.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant who wants to accuse another should first check five things in themselves and establish five things in themselves.
translation_text.an10.44:2.2: What five things should they check in themselves?
translation_text.an10.44:2.3: A mendicant who wants to accuse another should check this:
translation_text.an10.44:2.4: ‘Is my bodily behavior pure? Do I have pure bodily behavior that is impeccable and irreproachable?
translation_text.an10.44:2.5: Is this thing found in me or not?’
translation_text.an10.44:2.6: If it’s not, there will be people who say:
translation_text.an10.44:2.7: ‘Come on, venerable, train your own bodily behavior first.’
translation_text.an10.44:3.1: Furthermore, a mendicant who wants to accuse another should check this:
translation_text.an10.44:3.2: ‘Is my verbal behavior pure? Do I have pure verbal behavior that is impeccable and irreproachable?
translation_text.an10.44:3.3: Is this thing found in me or not?’
translation_text.an10.44:3.4: If it’s not, there will be people who say:
translation_text.an10.44:3.5: ‘Come on, venerable, train your own verbal behavior first.’
translation_text.an10.44:4.1: Furthermore, a mendicant who wants to accuse another should check this:
translation_text.an10.44:4.2: ‘Is my heart established in love for my spiritual companions, without resentment?
translation_text.an10.44:4.3: Is this thing found in me or not?’
translation_text.an10.44:4.4: If it’s not, there will be people who say:
translation_text.an10.44:4.5: ‘Come on, venerable, establish your heart in love for your spiritual companions first.’
translation_text.an10.44:5.1: Furthermore, a mendicant who wants to accuse another should check this:
translation_text.an10.44:5.2: ‘Am I very learned, remembering and keeping what I’ve learned? These teachings are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased, describing a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure. Am I very learned in such teachings, remembering them, rehearsing them, mentally scrutinizing them, and penetrating them theoretically?
translation_text.an10.44:5.3: Is this thing found in me or not?’
translation_text.an10.44:5.4: If it’s not, there will be people who say:
translation_text.an10.44:5.5: ‘Come on, venerable, memorize the scriptures first.’
translation_text.an10.44:6.1: Furthermore, a mendicant who wants to accuse another should check this:
translation_text.an10.44:6.2: ‘Have both monastic codes been passed down to me in detail, well analyzed, well mastered, and well evaluated in both the rules and accompanying material?
translation_text.an10.44:6.3: Is this thing found in me or not?’
translation_text.an10.44:6.4: If it’s not, and if they are unable to respond when asked “Venerable, where was this spoken by the Buddha?” there will be people who say:
translation_text.an10.44:6.6: ‘Come on, venerable, train in the monastic code first.’
translation_text.an10.44:6.7: These are the five things they should check in themselves.
translation_text.an10.44:7.1: What five things should they establish in themselves?
translation_text.an10.44:7.2: ‘I will speak at the right time, not at the wrong time.
translation_text.an10.44:7.3: I will speak truthfully, not falsely.
translation_text.an10.44:7.4: I will speak gently, not harshly.
translation_text.an10.44:7.5: I will speak beneficially, not harmfully.
translation_text.an10.44:7.6: I will speak lovingly, not from secret hate.’
translation_text.an10.44:7.7: These are the five things they should establish in themselves.
translation_text.an10.44:7.8: A mendicant who wants to accuse another should first check these five things in themselves and establish these five things in themselves.”
